detail: Deprecated exdqlmChecks replaced with exdqlmDiagnostics.
Deprecated y= usage removed from exdqlmPlot/compPlot/exdqlmForecast calls.
Example 2 manuscript workflow now uses LDVB and MCMC only; ISVB support artifacts were retired.
Benchmark Profile B (manuscript-matched backend) is active for manuscript runs; current MCMC backend options are exdqlm.use_cpp_mcmc=TRUE and exdqlm.cpp_mcmc_mode='fast'.
Lake Huron uses cached fits; ex1mcmc uses a dedicated high-iteration median MCMC chain, and runtime statements are profile-dependent (see ex1_run_summary).
Added LDVB diagnostic refit for convergence checks (p0=0.95, tol=0.01, n.samp=3000, max_iter=500, iter=359).
Sunspots LDVB discount-factor screen selects seasonal discount factor=0.95 by CRPS for this run profile; KL is reported alongside it.
Example 3 selected lambda=0.400 by finite CRPS over the documented grid.
Example 3 uses observed package BTflow and standardized NOI and AMO from climateIndices over 1987-01 to 2022-12.
Example 4 uses a sparse correlated-Gaussian regression benchmark with a target-quantile-centered Gaussian response model, so the true p0-quantile equals X beta at each fitted p0.
The static sparse benchmark uses the regularized horseshoe (RHS) prior with tau0 = 0.15, zeta2_fixed = 9, and an unshrunk intercept.
The p0=0.05 LDVB fit uses an expanded iteration budget; p0=0.25 and p0=0.50 use the standard Example 4 LDVB budget.
The tracked Example 4 dataset seed (20260718) was selected by the support-only ex4screen workflow using the p0=0.50 MCMC full-coverage criterion for the plotted slope coefficients.
Example 4 focuses on the general static exAL model; the AL special case remains available via al.ind = TRUE (static alias of dqlm.ind = TRUE).
All publication-set manuscript artifacts were targeted in this pipeline.
Exact runtime printouts in manuscript are historical and expected to differ.
Runtime values depend on hardware and backend settings; the Example 4 table reflects the standard-profile reproduction run recorded here.
Benchmark tables reported in the manuscript use backend Profile B; benchmark_backend_profiles.csv defines both disclosed benchmark profiles.
benchmark_environment.csv records CPU, R version, package/article state, backend options, seeds, and dataset sizes for the tracked benchmark run.
Automated reproduction outputs are isolated under analysis/manuscript; manuscript text updates are tracked separately in article4.tex.
